question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `--hiero.mirror.importer.parser.record.enabled=false` to `SpringApplication.run()`, disabling record file parsing entirely via `AbstractStreamFileParser.shouldParse()`, halting the processing of legitimate Hashgraph history while simultaneously enabling block stream parsing with a forged block source, effectively replacing the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `--hiero.mirror.importer.block.stream.maxBlockItems=1` to `SpringApplication.run()`, causing `BlockStreamVerifier.verify()` to reject all legitimate block files that contain more than 1 item, halting ingestion of real Hashgraph history and creating a denial-of-service condition that enables history manipulation through alternative sources?
